page: UpCloud DEV-2xCPU-4GB is a Developer family virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 2 shared vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 60 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance is positioned for entry-level workloads. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng fall into the bottom 10% category. Memory bandwidth tests, including both cached and uncached read/write operations, are characterized as weak, falling in the bottom 25%. However, single-threaded operations show moderate capability, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scoring in the middle 50% range. Geekbench single-core performance is average, though multi-core metrics, software compilation, and image processing remain in the bottom 25%.

The shared CPU allocation model presents a performance tradeoff, as resource contention may affect sustained compute-heavy tasks, as evidenced by the bottom 10% static web serving throughput. Qualitatively, this server offers a budget-friendly entry point within the Developer tier, trading high-throughput capabilities for resource density and basic utility. It is best suited for lightweight development environments, code testing, single-threaded utility scripts, and low-traffic staging applications rather than production databases or high-concurrency web serving.
description: UpCloud DEV-2xCPU-4GB is a Developer family virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 2 shared vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 60 GB local SSD. Benchmark data positions this instance in the lower performance tiers, with stress-ng CPU metrics in the bottom 10% and memory bandwidth in the bottom 25%. However, it demonstrates average single-threaded performance in gzip compression and Geekbench single-core tests. Due to its shared CPU allocation, the instance is subject to resource contention tradeoffs. It represents a cost-efficient option for non-production environments, making it suitable for software development, basic testing, and lightweight utility workloads.
og_description: UpCloud DEV-2xCPU-4GB is a Developer family virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a 60 GB local SSD, designed for entry-level development and testing workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud DEV-2xCPU-4GB is an entry-level KVM virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and a 60 GB SSD for development workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server with shared AMD EPYC processors and local SSD storage for basic development and testing.
bullet_points: Features 2 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory
Includes 60 GB of local SSD storage and 1 public IPv4 address
Delivers average single-threaded performance for basic compression and text tasks
Shared CPU allocation presents performance tradeoffs for sustained multi-core workloads
Suited for lightweight development, staging environments, and code testing
